title: Pentacalia rugosa
intro: Pentacalia rugosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it useful in the treatment of various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Pentacalia rugosa include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pentacalia rugosa include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, as well as to promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Pentacalia rugosa is classified within the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. The genus Pentacalia belongs to the tribe Senecioneae, a group of plants that are primarily found in the tropical and subtropical regions of the world. This genus is characterized by its unique leaf morphology and distribution of flowers. Pentacalia rugosa is a specific species within this genus and is known for its distinctive leaf characteristics. The classification of Pentacalia rugosa highlights the complex relationships within the Asteraceae family and the diversity of plant species within it.
